title: Community Relations: Advertising and Fundraising
description: This policy sets the rules for how outside advertising and fundraising activities are handled within the school district. It applies to businesses, organizations, and community groups that want to promote products, services, or fundraising campaigns through district channels such as school newsletters, websites, or events. Parents and community members should be aware of this policy because it ensures that any advertising or fundraising that reaches students and families meets the district's standards and reflects its core values.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy GKB (Local) were adopted unanimously at the November 18, 2025 Regular Board Meeting as part of a group adoption (Motion #7207). No specific language changes are described in the meeting minutes.
timeline_local.notes: Adopted as part of a group motion (#7207) along with BBE, CAA, CDA, CQB, EHB, and EHBB. Vote was 7-0. The specific redline changes are not described in these minutes pages.

timeline_local.notes: This is a table of contents / agenda slide (page 128) listing policies under 'Second Reading.' It does not contain redline text or specific policy language. Detailed changes would appear in subsequent pages of the packet for each individual policy.

timeline_local.summary: Policy GKB is being updated to add a new requirement that advertising must be aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with specific local policies (EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local). This provides an additional basis for rejecting advertising beyond the five existing criteria already listed in the policy.
changes_added: Advertising shall be rejected if not aligned and consistent with district values and in compliance with EMB Local, EFA Local, EFB Local, and EMI Local.

- **Trustee noted inconsistency:** Previous policy (DH) uses "nicotine" while this uses "tobacco." Requested language be made consistent — Dr. Vinson to research and update for second reading.

### GKB Local — Community Relations/Advertising and Fundraising
- Commercial advertising in district facilities must be consistent with district values.

**Note:** Policy CY was in the board packet but was **deferred to a future reading** (not discussed at this meeting).

timeline_local.notes: The added language appears in red/orange colored text on the scanned document, which in this context functions as the redline indicator for added text (rather than traditional underlining). The text appears after the five numbered criteria for rejecting advertising and before the statement prohibiting paid political advertising. Policy issued date shown as 2019-01-22, Version 1. Page number 270 in the packet, Policy Update Page Number 32.

description_local: This policy governs what types of advertising and fundraising activities are allowed on school district property and through district communications. It applies to outside organizations, businesses, and community groups that want to promote products, services, or fundraising campaigns through the district. Parents and community members should be aware that the district now has clear guidelines requiring all advertising to align with district values and meet specific standards, giving the school board greater control over the messages students and families are exposed to through school channels.
